back_translation: Now regarding the matter of spiritual gifts, all you brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be without knowledge.
You all know that, formerly when you all were Gentiles, you were led to mute idols, however anyone might lead you.
Therefore, I tell you all to know that no one who speaks by the Spirit of God will say, "Jesus is cursed," and no one is able to say, "Jesus is the (royal) Lord," except by the Holy Spirit.
There are gifts of many different kinds — but the same Spirit.
And there are services of many different kinds — but the same (royal) Lord.
And there are powerful workings of many different kinds — but the same God, who (royally) works all things in all people.
The manifestation of the Spirit is given to each one, for the common benefit.
Because to one person is given the utterance of wisdom by the Spirit; to another, the utterance of knowledge by the same Spirit.
To another, faith by the same Spirit; to another, gifts of healing illness, by that one same Spirit.
To another, the powerful workings; to another, prophecy; to another, the discerning of spirits; to another, the speaking of various languages; to another, the interpretation of languages.
All these things, the one same Spirit (royally) works as the doer; (royally) distributing to each person specifically according to His will.
Because the body is one, but it has many parts; and all the parts of the body, although there are many, are still one body. Like this is Christ also.
Because we all received baptism in one Spirit into one body — whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free people; and we all received the giving of one Spirit to drink.
Because the body does not consist of only one part, but consists of many parts.
If the foot were to say, "Because I am not the hand, therefore I am not part of the body," that does not make the foot not be a part of the body at all.
And if the ear were to say, "Because I am not the eye, therefore I am not part of the body," that does not make the ear not be a part of the body at all.
If the entire body were the eye, where would the hearing be? If the entire body were the hearing, where would the smelling be?
But now, God has (royally) arranged each part within the body according to what He (royally) willed.
If all of them were one part only, where would the body be?
But now there are many parts, but only one body.
The eye cannot say to the hand, "I do not need you," and the head cannot say to the feet, "I do not need you all."
On the contrary, the parts that seem to be weaker are actually those that cannot be done without.
And the parts that we think are of less honor, we cover with greater honor; and the parts that are not appropriate to be displayed, are treated with greater appropriateness.
While the parts of ours that look good have no need of this. But God has (royally) composed the body, (royally) giving more honor to the part that lacks.
So that there will not be division within the body, but that the parts will have concern for one another equally.
If one part suffers, all the other parts also suffer with it. If one part receives honor, all the other parts also rejoice with it.
Now you all are the body of Christ, and each person is one part of this body.

And God has (royally) appointed some in the church — first, apostles; second, prophets; third, teachers; after these, the workers of mighty deeds; gifts of healing illnesses; helping; managing-administration; and speaking of various languages.
Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work mighty deeds?
Do all have gifts of healing illnesses? Do all speak in various languages? Do all interpret languages?
But desire earnestly the greater gifts. And I myself will yet show to you all a way that is even more excellent than this.
